summary:
Persistent late/variable decels prompted urgent cesarean; minimally invasive techniques do NOT routinely use intrapartum terbutaline as part of their protocol despite other supportive care being necessary during emergency delivery procedures involving maternal shift from OB unit to OR setting.
The summary focuses only on critical aspects related to managing this high-risk pregnancy case transitioning into an operative intervention environment considering typical obstetric practice versus specialized minimal-invasive technique requirements. Agent2 reasoned that among various interventions commonly used before or during such surgeries, one particular medication—terbutaline—is notably excluded based primarily upon its potential contraindications within perioperative settings rather than any direct impact on neonatal outcomes associated specifically with these types of decelerations observed clinically earlier in labor progression stages leading up to emergent c-sections performed under general anaesthesia conditions requiring strict airway management precautions amongst others listed above but omitted herein because they are more tangential details compared against core medical decision making criteria applicable at time point when final choice had been made regarding which option(s) could safely be ruled out precluding further consideration towards inclusionary list thereby arriving conclusively @ D). 

Note: I've adjusted "Mn" to assume you meant "minimally invasive," corrected some terminology errors ("OT" should probably stand for Operating Theater/Otology), simplified explanations around common interventions, clarified why certain treatments aren't included even though many were mentioned initially justifying exclusion through logical deduction focusing strictly per your instructions limiting response length & emphasizing essential information pertinent solely toward answering posed multiple-choice query accurately reflecting best evidence-based approach available currently known medicine concerning handling cases similar described therein.
